channel.title: Retro Computing on jamesm.blog
channel.description: Recent content in Retro Computing on jamesm.blog

item.title: Copy Protection Wars: The Ingenious Schemes Of 1980s Software
item.description: A deep dive into the creative, bizarre, and often frustrating world of 1980s copy protection, from code wheels to lensloks.

item.title: When 8-bit Computers Taught An Entire Nation To Code
item.description: In the 1980s, a unique confluence of government policy, public broadcasting, and garage-built hardware turned the UK into a coding powerhouse. This is how it happened.

item.title: What the Amiga Got Right (That We're Still Copying)
item.description: Why the Amiga was 20 years ahead of its time, and why modern operating systems are still trying to copy its best ideas.

item.title: Compunet: Britain's Forgotten Pre-Internet Community
item.description: The story of Compunet: how a British BBS became one of the world's first online communities, years before the internet, and how it shaped British digital culture.

item.title: The Demoscene: Where Art Met Assembly
item.description: The story of the demoscene: where programmers turned constraint into creativity, and assembly language into art.

item.title: From BASIC in 1981 to Claude Code in 2026: What Programming Has Always Been About
item.description: A 45-year thread from typing BASIC on a ZX Spectrum to using Claude Code: what hasn't changed about programming, and what fundamentally has.

item.title: Favourite Museums
item.description: Personal list of favourite museums, particularly focused on computing, technology and natural history.

item.title: Favourite Computers, Consoles & Mobiles
item.description: Personal history and collection of favourite computers, gaming consoles, and mobile devices from the 1970s to present day.

item.title: Apple finally allows game emulators in the App Store
item.description: News and resources about Apple's decision to allow game emulators in the App Store, including Delta, Dolphin, and retro emulator apps.

item.title: Favourite Computer Games
item.description: Personal collection of favourite computer and console games across decades of gaming history.
